description: Cross-device clipboard and file transfer tool via a user-specified relay server. MVP supports macOS (Swift+SwiftUI) and Windows (C#+WPF) clients with TLS-encrypted transfer, chunked upload for large files, and TTL-based auto-cleanup.

userStories.title: Server: project scaffolding, health endpoint, and auth middleware
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Go server project initialized with routing, a /health endpoint, and Bearer Token auth middleware so all subsequent API work has a foundation.
acceptanceCriteria: Go module initialized with Gin dependency
GET /health returns JSON with version string and server uptime
Auth middleware reads Bearer token from Authorization header and rejects requests with 401 if token does not match ACCESS_TOKEN env var
Server reads config from environment variables: ACCESS_TOKEN, PORT (default 8080), STORAGE_PATH (default ./data), MAX_CLIP_SIZE_MB (default 500), TTL_HOURS (default 1)
go build succeeds with no errors

userStories.title: Server: SQLite schema and clip metadata model
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a SQLite database with a clips table so clip metadata can be persisted and queried.
acceptanceCriteria: SQLite database created at STORAGE_PATH/copy_everywhere.db on startup

clips table with columns: id (TEXT PK, 6-char alphanumeric), type (TEXT: text/image/file), filename (TEXT nullable), size_bytes (INTEGER), status (TEXT: ready/uploading/failed), created_at (DATETIME), expires_at (DATETIME), storage_path (TEXT)
Helper functions: CreateClip, GetClipByID, GetLatestClip, ListClips, DeleteClip
ID generation produces unique 6-char alphanumeric codes
go build succeeds with no errors
Tests pass

userStories.title: Server: clips API - upload, get metadata, get latest, download
userStories.description: As a client app, I need API endpoints to upload text/small files, retrieve clip metadata, get the latest clip, and download raw content.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/v1/clips accepts multipart/form-data with fields: type (text/image/file), content (file part), stores file to STORAGE_PATH/<id>/<filename>
POST /api/v1/clips returns JSON: {id, type, filename, size_bytes, created_at, expires_at}
POST /api/v1/clips rejects files exceeding MAX_CLIP_SIZE_MB with 413
GET /api/v1/clips/:id returns clip metadata JSON
GET /api/v1/clips/:id returns 404 for expired or non-existent clips
GET /api/v1/clips/latest returns the most recent non-expired clip metadata
GET /api/v1/clips/:id/raw streams the file content with correct Content-Type and Content-Disposition headers
GET /api/v1/clips/:id/raw returns 403 for clips with status=failed
go build succeeds with no errors
Tests pass

userStories.title: Server: chunked upload API
userStories.description: As a client app, I need chunked upload endpoints so large files can be uploaded in parts with resume support.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/v1/uploads/init accepts JSON {filename, size_bytes, chunk_size} and returns {upload_id, chunk_count}
Creates a clip record with status=uploading and a directory STORAGE_PATH/uploads/<upload_id>/
PUT /api/v1/uploads/:id/parts/:n accepts binary body and saves chunk to parts directory
PUT returns 409 if chunk already uploaded, 404 if upload_id not found
POST /api/v1/uploads/:id/complete merges all chunks into final file, updates clip status to ready, returns clip metadata with Clip ID
POST /api/v1/uploads/:id/complete returns 400 if any chunks are missing
GET /api/v1/uploads/:id/status returns {upload_id, received_parts: [1,3,5], total_parts, status}
go build succeeds with no errors
Tests pass

userStories.title: Server: TTL cleanup and health storage stats
userStories.description: As a server operator, I need expired clips auto-cleaned and storage usage visible in the health endpoint.
acceptanceCriteria: Background goroutine runs every 10 minutes scanning for clips where expires_at < now()
Expired clips: delete file from disk, delete row from SQLite
Failed uploads (status=uploading) follow the same TTL expiry and cleanup
GET /health response includes storage_used_bytes and clip_count fields
Cleanup logs number of deleted clips per run
go build succeeds with no errors

userStories.title: Server: Dockerfile and docker-compose.yml
userStories.description: As a server operator, I want to deploy the relay server with a single docker-compose up command.
acceptanceCriteria: Multi-stage Dockerfile: build stage (golang:1.22) and runtime stage (alpine)
docker-compose.yml with service 'relay' exposing port 8080, volume mount for data persistence
docker-compose.yml includes environment variables: ACCESS_TOKEN, MAX_CLIP_SIZE_MB, TTL_HOURS, STORAGE_PATH
docker-compose up --build starts the server successfully
.dockerignore excludes unnecessary files

userStories.title: macOS: Xcode project, MenuBarExtra shell, and server config UI
userStories.description: As a macOS user, I want a menu bar app where I can configure my relay server connection.
acceptanceCriteria: SwiftUI app with MenuBarExtra (macOS 13+), no Dock icon by default
Menu bar icon shows app popover panel on click
First launch shows config screen: Host URL text field, Access Token secure field
Config saved to macOS Keychain using Security framework
Test Connection button calls GET /health and shows latency or error message
Config errors show specific messages: connection refused, auth failed (401), timeout
Xcode build succeeds with no errors
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: macOS: send clipboard text
userStories.description: As a macOS user, I want to send my current clipboard text to the relay server with one click.
acceptanceCriteria: Main panel shows current clipboard text preview (max 500 chars, truncated with ellipsis)
Send Clipboard button reads NSPasteboard.general.string and POSTs to /api/v1/clips with type=text
On success: displays Clip ID and expiry time
On failure: shows error message with Retry button
Send button disabled when clipboard is empty
Xcode build succeeds with no errors
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: macOS: receive clipboard text
userStories.description: As a macOS user, I want to receive the latest text from the relay server into my clipboard.
acceptanceCriteria: Receive Latest button calls GET /api/v1/clips/latest
If text clip received: writes to NSPasteboard.general, shows macOS notification 'Copied to clipboard'
If no content available: shows 'No content available or expired' message
Manual Clip ID input field: enter a 6-char ID to GET /api/v1/clips/:id/raw for specific content
Xcode build succeeds with no errors
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: macOS: send small file (< 50MB)
userStories.description: As a macOS user, I want to send a file by dragging it into the app or picking it from a file dialog.
acceptanceCriteria: Drop zone area in main panel accepts dragged files from Finder
Choose File button opens NSOpenPanel file picker
Upload progress bar shows percentage and speed (e.g. '45% - 2.3 MB/s')
On completion: displays Clip ID, filename, file size, and expiry time
Files >= 50MB are rejected with message directing to chunked upload (handled in next story)
Xcode build succeeds with no errors
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: macOS: send large file with chunked upload
userStories.description: As a macOS user, I want to send large files (>= 50MB) with chunk-based upload and pause/resume support.
acceptanceCriteria: Files >= 50MB automatically use chunked upload (10MB chunks)
Progress shows chunk progress (e.g. 'Chunk 3/24') and overall percentage
Pause button cancels current chunk upload task and records completed chunk numbers
Resume button queries GET /api/v1/uploads/:id/status for received parts and resumes from next missing chunk
On completion: POST /api/v1/uploads/:id/complete called, displays Clip ID
Xcode build succeeds with no errors
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: macOS: receive and download file
userStories.description: As a macOS user, I want to download a file from the relay server to a local directory.
acceptanceCriteria: Enter Clip ID shows file metadata: filename, size, type, upload time
Download button opens NSSavePanel for choosing save location
Download progress bar shows percentage and speed
Large files download via streaming (URLSessionDownloadTask)
On completion: reveals file in Finder using NSWorkspace.shared.activateFileViewerSelecting
Clips with status=failed show 'Upload incomplete - download unavailable'
Xcode build succeeds with no errors
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: macOS: send history list and management
userStories.description: As a macOS user, I want to see my sent items history so I can copy Clip IDs or clean up old entries.
acceptanceCriteria: History list in main panel shows locally-stored records: Clip ID, type icon, timestamp, expired status
History stored in UserDefaults or local SQLite (not on server)
Click a record copies its Clip ID to clipboard
Expired items shown in gray with 'Expired' label
Failed uploads shown with 'Upload Failed' label
Swipe-to-delete or delete button removes entry from local history
Xcode build succeeds with no errors
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Windows: WPF project, system tray, and server config UI
userStories.description: As a Windows user, I want a system tray app where I can configure my relay server connection.
acceptanceCriteria: WPF app (.NET 8) with NotifyIcon system tray, left-click opens main window
App starts minimized to tray, no taskbar icon when minimized
First launch shows config screen: Host URL text field, Access Token password field
Config saved to Windows Credential Manager via CredentialManager NuGet
Test Connection button calls GET /health and shows latency or error
Config errors show specific messages: connection refused, auth failed (401), timeout
dotnet build succeeds with no errors

userStories.title: Windows: send and receive clipboard text
userStories.description: As a Windows user, I want to send my clipboard text and receive text from the relay server.
acceptanceCriteria: Main panel shows clipboard text preview (max 500 chars, truncated)
Send Clipboard reads System.Windows.Clipboard.GetText() and POSTs to /api/v1/clips
On success: displays Clip ID and expiry time
Receive Latest calls GET /api/v1/clips/latest, writes text to Clipboard.SetText()
Shows Windows toast notification 'Copied to clipboard' on receive
Manual Clip ID input for specific clip retrieval
dotnet build succeeds with no errors

userStories.title: Windows: send and receive files with chunked upload support
userStories.description: As a Windows user, I want to send and receive files with the same capabilities as the macOS client.
acceptanceCriteria: Drop zone accepts files dragged from Explorer
Choose File button opens OpenFileDialog
Small files (< 50MB) upload via single POST with progress bar
Large files (>= 50MB) use chunked upload with pause/resume
Progress bar shows percentage and speed for both upload and download
Download: enter Clip ID, view metadata, SaveFileDialog for save location
Download completion opens Explorer and selects the file (Process.Start explorer.exe /select)
dotnet build succeeds with no errors

userStories.title: Windows: send history list and management
userStories.description: As a Windows user, I want to see my sent items history, copy Clip IDs, and manage entries.
acceptanceCriteria: History list shows locally-stored records: Clip ID, type icon, timestamp, expired/failed status
History stored in local SQLite database in AppData
Click a record copies Clip ID to clipboard
Expired items shown grayed out with 'Expired' label
Failed uploads shown with 'Upload Failed' label
Delete button removes entry from local history
dotnet build succeeds with no errors
